refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly focus on the latency metric and its impact on safe teleoperation, clarifying the performance requirement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms during teleoperation sessions.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link with minimal packet loss and jitter.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking data exchange with low overhead.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize communication protocol stack for low latency (e.g., UDP-based custom protocol).
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes to reduce network hops and processing delays.
DP3: Use prioritized network traffic and QoS policies for teleoperation data.
DP4: Design telemetry and tasking data formats for minimal size and processing overhead.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Optimize communication protocol stack for low latency (e.g., UDP-based custom protocol).

potential_sources: Telemetry data format changes impacting tasking interface
coupling_check.notes: DP1 focuses on communication protocol optimization which is logically decoupled from other system functions but requires coordination with network QoS.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions meet agreed baseline for latency measurement.
Risk of protocol incompatibility with existing vehicle communication modules.
Risk that protocol optimization alone may not meet latency under all conditions.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge computing (DP2) if latency targets are not met in testing.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for implementing teleoperation communication protocols, managing telemetry and tasking data exchange, and ensuring latency targets.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exchange of real-time vehicle state and sensor data between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and control instructions sent from Teleoperation to Vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection supporting data transmission for teleoperation.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad system scope, low containment specificity for communication protocol.
Vehicle handles telemetry but not protocol optimization; moderate containment.
Primary module for teleoperation communication protocol and latency management.
Safety Services focus on alerts and policies, minimal role in communication latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP1 to Teleoperation module centralizes communication protocol logic, minimizing diffusion and improving latency control.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle module due to telemetry and tasking data exchange.
Dependency on network link quality outside Teleoperation control.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Review interface definitions to ensure minimal overhead and clear data ownership between Teleoperation and Vehicle.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (communication protocol optimization) directly addresses the refined FR latency requirement with clear verification approach.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists at Teleoperation-Vehicle interface due to telemetry and tasking data exchange.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows high containment score for DP1, indicating good architectural fit.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end using telemetry interface and network link monitoring.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear ownership of latency-related functionality in Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface protocols between Teleoperation and Vehicle to reduce overhead.
Plan for fallback or mitigation strategies if network link quality degrades.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, interface overhead and network dependency risks require refinement before proceeding.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link with minimal packet loss and jitter.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking data exchange with low overhead.

revise.why: Refine interface definitions and containment boundaries to reduce diffusion and improve latency control.

assumptions: Teleoperation module can implement optimized protocol.
open_risks: Interface overhead between Teleoperation and Vehicle.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under representative network conditions.
Interface performance profiling between Teleoperation and Vehicle.
